title: inga multijuga subsp. aestuariorum
intro: Inga multijuga subsp. aestuariorum, also known as the Estuary Inga, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Inga multijuga subsp. aestuariorum include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
